how_to_apply: Interested candidates may apply on plain paper with detailed bio-data along with self-attested copies of certificates to the address of the Principal Investigator by post on or before 10.07.2026. Soft copy of the application should also be sent to [email protected].
Shortlisted candidates will be called for a written exam followed by interview. No TA/ DA will be paid for attending the interview.
important_instructions: The position is purely temporary and co-terminus with the project. Fellowship disbursement is subject to the release of grant from MoES. Candidates will be selected based on written test and interview. No TA/DA will be provided for attending the selection process.

content_sections.heading: MKU JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification
content_sections.text: Madurai Kamaraj University has invited applications for the position of Junior Research Fellow (JRF) on a purely temporary basis in the MoES funded research project titled “Studies on Marine Bioactive Metabolites from Deep Sea Actinomycetes and their Molecular Mechanism against Fungal Plant Pathogens”. The project reference number is F. No. MOES/PAMC/DOM/145/2023 (E-14628). Download Madurai Kamaraj University JRF Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: MKU JRF Recruitment 2026 Overview
content_sections.text: Madurai Kamaraj University is recruiting 01 JRF under MoES project with M.Sc. qualification. Last date of application is 10 July 2026.

rows.details: First Class in M.Sc. Microbial Gene Technology / Botany / Zoology / Microbiology / Genomics / Biotechnology and all other Life Science courses.

i) Qualification in National Level Tests such as CSIR-NET, UGC-NET, SET, GATE, ASRB-NET. ii) Two years of research experience in Plant Microbe Interaction, Biological control, and Bioactive molecules purification & characterization.
One year or co-terminus with the project.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for MKU JRF Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Interested candidates may apply on plain paper with detailed bio-data along with self-attested copies of certificates to the address of the Principal Investigator by post on or before 10.07.2026 . Soft copy of the application should also be sent to [email protected] . Shortlisted candidates will be called for a written exam followed by interview. No TA/ DA will be paid for attending the interview.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: The position is purely temporary and co-terminus with the project. Fellowship disbursement is subject to the release of grant from MoES. Candidates will be selected based on written test and interview. No TA/DA will be provided for attending the selection process.

faq.question: Q1. What is Madurai Kamaraj University JRF Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Madurai Kamaraj University has invited applications for one Junior Research Fellow position in a Ministry of Earth Sciences (MoES) funded project on marine bioactive metabolites from deep sea actinomycetes. The project focuses on their molecular mechanism against fungal plant pathogens.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for MKU JRF 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for submission of application for Madurai Kamaraj University JRF Recruitment is 10th July 2026. Candidates can send both hard copy by post and soft copy via email.

faq.question: Q3. What is the fellowship amount for JRF at Madurai Kamaraj University?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive Rs. 37,000 + 16% HRA per month as fellowship. The position is purely temporary and the duration is one year or co-terminus with the project.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for Madurai Kamaraj University JRF Recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must have First Class M.Sc. degree in Microbiology, Biotechnology, Microbial Gene Technology, Botany, Zoology, Genomics or any Life Science subject. Candidates with NET/GATE qualification and research experience in Plant Microbe Interaction or Bioactive molecules will be preferred.

faq.question: Q5. How to apply for MKU JRF under MoES project?
faq.answer: Interested candidates should send their application on plain paper with bio-data and self-attested certificates to the Principal Investigator by post. A soft copy must also be emailed to [email protected] before 10 July 2026.

faq.question: Q6. Is NET qualification mandatory for this JRF post?
faq.answer: No, NET qualification is not mandatory but it is highly desirable. Candidates with CSIR-NET, UGC-NET, GATE or other national level tests will get preference during selection.
